verdict_reasoning: LYN is a non-receptor Src-family tyrosine kinase anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via dual lipidation (myristoylation and palmitoylation). It lacks a transmembrane domain and has no extracellular-facing ectodomains. While LYN transits to lipid rafts on the PM and is recruited to activated surface receptors, it functions exclusively from the intracellular side as a signaling hub. The protein body itself is not accessible from the extracellular face. No documented cell-state-induced ecto-presentation, no tissue-restricted surface display, no lysosomal-exocytosis trafficking to expose it externally, no dual localization with outer-leaflet exposure, and no documented stable post-translational tethering to a surface TM partner that would expose the Lyn body externally.
